entry.title: SuccessFactors Business Rules: Now it's time for a deep dive in the format() function!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to share with you some tips around the format function. It’s one of the most important functions used in Business Rules, as it allows you to combine different elements into meaningful text. For example, you can use it to merge a country code (e.g., DE), a business unit name (e.g., SMB), and a number (e.g., 111) into a single value like DESMB111.

The format function is also useful for tasks such as concatenation, adding leading zeros to codes, formatting dates, and more. For a deeper dive, check out the Implementing Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors document in the Help Portal.

Here are some examples: 

Simple Format: Let’s create a basic template. In this example, we are generating a position code:

  • % → start of the formatter
  • d → conversion digit

Upper Letter format [Strings: %s vs %S]: Some customers would like to store the first and last name information in uppercase.

As you can see above, the function has the following behavior:

  • %s → prints the string as-is
  • %S → converts the entire string to uppercase

Different Arguments [%N$] : This function can also be used to concatenate information from different fields. I personally prefer using it because it makes it easy to include elements like spaces between words.

  • %1$s → first argument (First Name)
  • %2$s → second argument (Last Name)

Adding leading zeros: In some cases, it’s necessary to add leading zeros to an external code. For example, a position code might need to be 8 digits long and generated sequentially.

  • 08 → represents the max number of digits that should be considered

Substring: This function can also be used to extract a substring, such as when defaulting the initials.

  • .1 → represents the number of chars that will be considered from the text

Rounding: The format function can also be used to display a decimal value with a specific number of decimal places.

  • .2 → represents the number of decimal places that should be considered from the calculation
  • f → floating type

Date/Time formatting: You can also use the function to format information based on date and time.

In terms of date/time formats here is a small list of the most used formatters:

FormatterDescriptionExample

%tY

Year (4 digits)

2026

%ty

Year (2 digits)

26

%tm

Month (01–12)

04

%td

Day (01–31)

06

%tB

Full month name

April

%tb

Short month name

Apr

%tA

Full weekday

Monday

%ta

Short weekday

Mon

%tH

Hour (00–23)

14

%tI

Hour (01–12)

02

%tM

Minute

35

%tS

Seconds

20

%tp

am/pm

pm

%tF

ISO date

2026-04-06

%tD

US date

04/06/26

%tc

Full date/time

Mon Apr 06 14:35:20 UTC 2026

%tr

12-hour time

02:35:20 PM

%tT

24-hour time

14:35:20

I hope this information has been helpful and encourages you to explore our standard functions to streamline your own implementation projects! As always, please discuss with your customers the pros and cons of adopting such a function in your projects.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (002) - OSの最新セキュリティパッチ適用
entry.content:
OSに最新のセキュリティパッチを適用し、システムの安全性を維持することは、システム運用において重要なタスクの一つです。

本記事では、SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionをご利用の際に、OSの最新セキュリティパッチ適用を依頼する方法をご紹介します。

 

未適用のOSセキュリティパッチの確認

 

SAP for Meへログイン後、以下の手順で確認できます。
「Services & Support」→「Private Cloud Workspace」→「Risk Management」セクションの「Security Patches」→「Operating System」タブを選択し、対象のSIDをクリックしてください。

また、以下のURLへアクセスすることで、適用が必要なセキュリティパッチを確認することも可能です。
URL: https://me.sap.com/privcloudsecuritypatches#?variant=standard 

下記は、サンプル画面となります。

 

利用するSRテンプレート

 

OSのセキュリティパッチ適用を依頼する際は、以下のSRテンプレートを使用します。
SRテンプレート：Apply latest Security Patch to OS (Linux, Windows)

下記は、本記事作成時点におけるSR作成画面です。

 

Creating with Advance Scheduling：NO　※特別な理由がない限り、デフォルト値のままご使用ください。

Set system messages(for ABAP systems): ABAPシステムにおいて、メンテナンス期間中に表示するシステムメッセージを設定するかどうかを指定する項目です。


注意事項

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editionでは、以下のように複数のシステムコンポーネントが提供されています。

  • SAP Cloud Connector
  • Web Dispatcher
  • S/4HANA（AP Server + SAP HANA）
    など

SAP HANAのSIDを選択してSRを作成した場合、AP ServerおよびSAP HANAのOSに対してセキュリティパッチが適用されます。
一方で、SAP Cloud ConnectorおよびWeb Dispatcherについては、それぞれのSIDごとにSRを作成する必要があります。


まとめ


システムを脆弱性から保護するため、OSのセキュリティパッチの定期的な適用を推奨いたします。
なお、適用作業にはダウンタイムが伴うため、事前にメンテナンス時間を確保した上で、計画的に実施することをご検討ください。

entry.title: Re: How to Get a View of Supplier Payments and Customer C...
entry.content:
Dear @Jessicaaguilar1,

The standard functionality for this requirement is Cash Flow Analyzer with drilldown to Check Cash Flow Items. SAP documents that Cash Flow Analyzer supports analysis over days, weeks, months, quarters or years and that you can navigate to Check Cash Flow Items for the underlying line-item details. SAP also documents that One Exposure from Operations derives forecasted cash flows for receivables and payables based on the net due date or cash discount date

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/573ee5f7021f4f6ee10000000a42189c.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/cd6705281eef4230b81ef5a7b1ec54ee.html

The limitation is that SAP delivers this as a combined liquidity forecast with drilldown, not as a separate predefined AP/AR weekly matrix with a custom side-by-side layout for supplier invoices and customer invoices, however there is a supported workaround. SAP documents released CDS content for Cash and Liquidity Management, including Cash Position and Liquidity Forecast Query (C_LiquidityPositionQuery) and Cash Flow Query. If you needs a specific weekly presentation beyond the standard app layout the defensible SAP-standard approach is to build the report on top of this released cash/liquidity analytical content rather than look for another standard operational app

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/be968afbde79451abb255fe123b3a17b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/17a419741df942cda71c2b1689c1b8f5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/05084020890140aab5bba044dd5491e6.html

If SAP Analytics Cloud is in scope, SAP also documents Liquidity Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud based on integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud data sources. That is the stronger SAP-standard option when the requirement is not only to review the standard forecast but also to present it in a more tailored planning/reporting format

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/39e81b8f567d415185768de918b5092d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/42093f14b43c485fbe3adbbe81eff6c8/b834ee07a87042c1a163af1869472dd8.html

Summary:

The standard app is Cash Flow Analyzer + Check Cash Flow Items. If the customer needs the same data in a stricter weekly report layout, the supported workaround is custom analytics on the released cash/liquidity CDS query content and optionally SAP Analytics Cloud if that is part of the solution scope

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: CLEAR ITAB or REFRESH ITAB
entry.content:
Dear @Dipeshhhh21,

In modern ABAP, CLEAR itab is the recommended statement and REFRESH itab should not be used in new code. SAP documents that REFRESH itab is obsolete and that for an internal table without a header line, REFRESH itab has the same effect as CLEAR itab more generally, SAP describes REFRESH itab as equivalent to CLEAR itab. SAP also states that tables with header lines are obsolete and forbidden in classes, which is why using REFRESH instead of CLEAR is obsolete as well

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_752_index_htm/7.52/en-US/abaprefresh_itab.htm

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABAPCLEAR.html

So:

  • CLEAR itab: deletes all rows of the internal table and keeps the initial memory requirement reserved. SAP explicitly notes this can even be beneficial when you will fill the table again

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABAPCLEAR.html

  • REFRESH itab: for normal modern internal tables, effectively the same as CLEAR itab but SAP classifies it as obsolete syntax
  • FREE itab: deletes all rows and releases the complete memory area used by the rows, including the initial memory requirement. SAP positions this for cases where memory really should be released as much as possible

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/ABAPFREE_DATAOBJECT.html

Summary:

CLEAR itab is better in modern ABAP, not because it is functionally stronger than REFRESH but because it is the current, non-obsolete, standard statement. REFRESH gives no advantage in modern code. If memory release is the real requirement then the better statement is not REFRESH but FREE itab

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Joule Exceeds Message Size 10KB
entry.content:
Help!

After integrating Joule with S/4HANA Private Cloud. I'm experiencing inconsistency with Joule’s responses on Fiori.

For example, when I ask Joule, “Find me suppliers containing XXX,” it works as expected. However, when I ask the same question for a different supplier, such as “Find me suppliers containing YYY,” Joule stops responding. It thinks for a bit and then returns nothing.

I then created a Joule Skill in Joule Studio to recreate the same scenario and ran into the same issue, but with better logging.

Based on the logs, the response says that the message size exceeded the 10KB limit. 

So, this limit is everywhere? If so, how can we increase the limit?

I know with building agents you can use $top or pagination or anything to stay under. But what about the actual Joule with S/4HANA Private?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: POD confirmation needed after billing due to Carta Porte process - how to handle VLPOD? - Mexico
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I would like to ask for guidance regarding a process design issue in SAP SD / LE for Mexico.

In the standard SAP process, Proof of Delivery (POD) with transaction VLPOD is normally used before billing, since the confirmed POD quantity can become the basis for invoicing.

However, in Mexico we have a legal/fiscal process constraint:

  • First, we need to create the billing document
  • Then we generate the CFDI / Carta Porte complement
  • Only after that can the transportation legally leave
  • Finally, once the customer actually receives the goods, we can confirm the delivery

Because of this sequence, the standard POD logic does not fit well, since the billing must already exist before the physical delivery is confirmed.

Our question is:

How can this scenario be handled in SAP when POD confirmation is required only after billing has already been created?

More specifically, we would like to understand:

  1. Is there any standard SAP approach to allow POD confirmation after billing?
  2. Can VLPOD still be used in some way only as a delivery confirmation step, without affecting the billing basis?
  3. Has anyone implemented this scenario in Mexico, especially where billing is mandatory first in order to generate Carta Porte?

We are trying to find the best design that:

  • complies with the Mexican legal requirement,
  • allows billing before transportation,
  • and still lets us confirm the actual delivery afterwards.

Any recommendations regarding standard configuration, best practice, or enhancement approach would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Sustainability Control Tower for BRSR - India
entry.content:
Hi Narendra, I hope you're doing great.

Great question! SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) does support the BRSR (Business Responsibility & Sustainability Reporting) framework through its Qualitative Metrics feature, available since the March 2025 release.

Using the Manage Metrics application, you can create custom qualitative metrics aligned with BRSR by defining:

  • Reporting Standard → BRSR

  • Disclosure Requirement → specific Section and Principle (e.g., Section-C, Principle-2)

  • Data Point → Essential or Leadership Indicators

  • Reference Type → Narrative, Semi-Narrative, or Boolean

Once metrics are created, data can be maintained via the Manage Qualitative ESG Data application.

Please note that SAP does not currently provide out-of-the-box BRSR content (SAP-provided qualitative metrics are ESRS-aligned). However, the platform's extensibility fully supports BRSR requirements through custom configuration.

For a detailed step-by-step walkthrough covering both ESRS and BRSR framework setup in SCT — including Sustainability Matter mapping, Disclosure References, and Data Points specific to BRSR — I'd recommend the following SAP Community blog, which covers exactly your use case:

Managing Qualitative Metrics in SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) for ESRS and BRSR Framework — by Pardhasaradhi Reddy Chelikam

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Valuated Assembly Item Usage Effects on CO FI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I'm trying to implement a assembly processing to my customer where they are a buy and sell company but sometimes they need to assemble some of materials that they have procured and deliver them. Which is a perfect enviroment from my knowledge where they also want to implement Project Systems and they don't want to implement Production Planning module. I have gone through the process, but i have one topic that im stuck with which is the relationship or maybe the best word is balance between the;

- GR Material's value or maybe COGS value in another word (Please correct me if it can differ)
-  Raw Material Consumption + Activity Confirmations


The GR Material's cost is written a credit to the Network Activity but as far as i know since I'm a PS consultant not a FICO consultant, these values needs to be settled to the Material at the period end once GR has been done if material is Standart Price Material which most of them my customer wants them to be.

Since I cant settle the costs from Network Activity, Network or WBS Element to Material directly, how should i be implementing this functionality for Semi-Finished or Finished goods with Standart Price where i dont break the system at the end for Controlling and Financial side?

Am I missing something where actually a Settlement Rule to Material can be auto generated? I have tried TECO after Final Confirmation, nothing happened. I have even tried to create settlement rules from BAPI for Material which CJ88 just doesn't settle anyway.

Or was this process was designed as these semi-finished or Finished goods should be implemented as Moving Avarage Price. 

Or Is there another period end allocation method where we should go to rather then directly settling everything to Material?

I would really appreciate the support on this matter since I have been doing an R&D on this topic for so long and quite stuck on this topic. I have putted an example screenshot of the network activity after the GR which i believe should be settled to Material.

Thanks And Regards,
Mert.

entry.title: Re: Unable to adapt standard Fiori Travel Request app – m...
entry.content:
Hello.

For adaptation or adaptability, I typically check the Fiori Launchpad Library to see if the app can support extensibility.  The Adapt UI only supports what the Fiori app can provide.

So with this said, I will rely on the main manual of the Travel Request app.

Assuming your My Request App is this Fiori App ID 

App ID
F0409 per URL Link:  https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0409')/S20OP 

then their main manual is:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f4153779fcb742c094bb6157c0ff1cbe/c7a57f583c630a02e10000000a44147b.html?version=2020.002 

From an Adapt UI perspective, another way to check is to find the manifest.json of the actual Fiori app.  in that manifest.json see if the parameter isAdaptationAllowed = true - you may search that too in the Network HAR trace or search for that keyword: isAdaptationAllowed. But again, not all Fiori apps support adaptation and the team cannot simply assumed all FIori apps support it.
So from our perspective, double check the app documentation.  If it says you can do it, you can do it.  Otherwise, most likely you can assumed you can't.

Regards,
Mark

entry.title: MIGO - Tornar MKPF-BKTXT obrigatório e preenchimento automático (movimentos 542/ZRT)
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Estou implementando uma validação na transação MIGO relacionada ao campo Texto de cabeçalho (MKPF-BKTXT / GOHEAD-BKTXT).

Requisito de negócio

Para os tipos de movimento 542 e ZRT, o campo Texto de cabeçalho deve ser obrigatório.

O sistema deve:

  1. Identificar o documento de material de origem (movimento 541) com base no material informado na MIGO (via tabela MSEG).
  2. Recuperar a nota fiscal correspondente (tabelas J_1BNFLIN / J_1BNFDOC).
  3. Preencher automaticamente o campo MKPF-BKTXT com o número da NF.

Caso não exista NF:

  • O campo deve permanecer obrigatório para preenchimento manual.

Implementação atual

Estou utilizando a BAdI MB_MIGO_BADI:

  • PAI_HEADER → cálculo do valor do BKTXT
  • CHECK_HEADER → validação de obrigatoriedade

Problema

Apesar do BKTXT estar sendo calculado corretamente:

  • O valor não é refletido de forma consistente na tela (GOHEAD-BKTXT) antes do POST
  • A validação no CHECK_HEADER pode disparar erro, pois o campo aparenta estar preenchido com dados não possíveis como a validação.

Dúvidas

Qual é a abordagem recomendada na MIGO para:

  1. Preencher automaticamente o GOHEAD-BKTXT e garantir que o valor seja exibido na tela antes da gravação?
  2. Realizar a validação corretamente sem conflito com o ciclo de processamento da MIGO?

A BAdI MB_MIGO_BADI é suficiente para atualização de tela?

ℹ️ Informações adicionais

  • Customizing de seleção de campos (SPRO) já configurado
  • Escopo restrito à transação MIGO (Enjoy)

Desenvolvimento no method : IF_EX_MB_MIGO_BADI~PAI_HEADER

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Include          ZINMM_MIGO_BKTXT_FILL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_goitem>    TYPE goitem,
               <fs_gohead>    TYPE gohead,
               <fs_bwart_scr> TYPE any,
               <fs_bktxt_scr> TYPE any.

DATA: lv_bwart  TYPE bwart,
      lv_mblnr  TYPE mseg-mblnr,
      lv_mjahr  TYPE mseg-mjahr,
      lv_docnum TYPE j_1bnflin-docnum,
      lv_nfenum TYPE j_1bnfdoc-nfenum,
      lv_series TYPE j_1bnfdoc-series,
      lv_refkey TYPE j_1bnflin-refkey,
      lv_bktxt  TYPE mkpf-bktxt.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 1. Controle de execução
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CHECK sy-ucomm = 'ENTR' OR sy-ucomm = 'BU' OR sy-ucomm = 'OK_GO' OR sy-ucomm = 'OK_POST'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 2. Recuperar dados da MIGO
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GOITEM')              TO <fs_goitem>.
ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GOHEAD')             TO <fs_gohead>.
ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GODEFAULT_TV-BWART') TO <fs_bwart_scr>.

CHECK <fs_goitem> IS ASSIGNED.
CHECK <fs_gohead> IS ASSIGNED.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 3. BWART
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
IF <fs_bwart_scr> IS ASSIGNED
AND <fs_bwart_scr> IS NOT INITIAL.

  lv_bwart = <fs_bwart_scr>.

ELSEIF <fs_goitem> IS ASSIGNED
AND <fs_goitem>-bwart IS NOT INITIAL.

  lv_bwart = <fs_goitem>-bwart.

ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 4. Executar somente para 542/ZRT
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CHECK lv_bwart = '542'   OR lv_bwart = 'ZRT'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 5. Não sobrescrever
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CHECK <fs_gohead>-bktxt IS INITIAL.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 6. Determinar documento origem
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CLEAR: lv_mblnr, lv_mjahr.

IF <fs_goitem>-smbln IS NOT INITIAL
AND <fs_goitem>-sjahr IS NOT INITIAL.

  SELECT SINGLE mblnr, mjahr
    FROM mseg
    INTO (@lv_mblnr, @LV_mjahr)
   WHERE mblnr = @<fs_goitem>-smbln
     AND mjahr = @<fs_goitem>-sjahr.

ELSEIF <fs_goitem>-ebeln IS NOT INITIAL.

  SELECT mblnr, mjahr
    FROM mseg
    UP TO 1 ROWS
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_mseg_ref)
    WHERE ebeln = @<fs_goitem>-ebeln
    ORDER BY mjahr DESCENDING, mblnr DESCENDING.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    READ TABLE lt_mseg_ref INTO DATA(ls_mseg_ref) INDEX 1.
    lv_mblnr = ls_mseg_ref-mblnr.
    lv_mjahr = ls_mseg_ref-mjahr.
  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

CHECK lv_mblnr IS NOT INITIAL
  AND lv_mjahr IS NOT INITIAL.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 7. REFKEY
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CONDENSE lv_mblnr NO-GAPS.
CONDENSE lv_mjahr NO-GAPS.
CONCATENATE lv_mblnr lv_mjahr INTO lv_refkey.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 8. Buscar NF
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
SELECT SINGLE docnum
  FROM j_1bnflin
  INTO @LV_docnum
 WHERE refkey = @LV_refkey.

CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

SELECT SINGLE nfenum, series
  FROM j_1bnfdoc
  INTO (@lv_nfenum, @LV_series)
 WHERE docnum = @LV_docnum.

CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 9. Montar BKTXT
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CHECK lv_nfenum IS NOT INITIAL
  AND lv_series IS NOT INITIAL.

SHIFT lv_nfenum LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.
SHIFT lv_series LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.

CONCATENATE lv_nfenum lv_series INTO lv_bktxt.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 10. NORMALIZAÇÃO
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CONDENSE lv_bktxt NO-GAPS.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 11. Preencher memória + tela
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
EXPORT lv_bktxt = lv_bktxt TO MEMORY ID 'ZMIGO_BKTXT'.

<fs_gohead>-bktxt = lv_bktxt.

ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GOHEAD-BKTXT') TO <fs_bktxt_scr>.
IF <fs_bktxt_scr> IS ASSIGNED.
  <fs_bktxt_scr> = lv_bktxt.
ENDIF.

 Desenvolvimento no method : IF_EX_MB_MIGO_BADI~CHECK_HEADER

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Include          ZINMM_MIGO_BKTXT_CHECK

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_bwart_scr> TYPE any,
               <fs_gohead>    TYPE gohead.

DATA: lv_bwart TYPE bwart,
      lv_bktxt TYPE mkpf-bktxt.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 1. Recuperar dados
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GODEFAULT_TV-BWART') TO <fs_bwart_scr>.
ASSIGN ('(SAPLMIGO)GOHEAD')             TO <fs_gohead>.

IF <fs_bwart_scr> IS ASSIGNED.
  lv_bwart = <fs_bwart_scr>.
ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 2. Recuperar BKTXT
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
IF <fs_gohead> IS ASSIGNED.
  lv_bktxt = <fs_gohead>-bktxt.
ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 3. Fallback memória
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
IF lv_bktxt IS INITIAL.
  IMPORT lv_bktxt = lv_bktxt FROM MEMORY ID 'ZMIGO_BKTXT'.
ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 4. NORMALIZAÇÃO
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CONDENSE lv_bktxt NO-GAPS.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* 5. Validação
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
IF lv_bwart = '542'
OR lv_bwart = 'ZRT'.

  IF lv_bktxt IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE e001(zmm).
  ENDIF.

  IF lv_bktxt CN '0123456789'.
    MESSAGE e002(zmm).
  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

Obs.: No debug o código está sendo preenchido totalmente e não aparenta erro.

Porém na tela o campo TXT.CABEÇALHO (<fs_gohead>-bktxt) não aparece preenchido e ainda apresenta a minha própria validação, dizendo que tem caracteres que não podem ter.

Agradeço desde já qualquer orientação ou boas práticas sobre esse cenário.

Obrigado!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud in Business Data Cloud Achieves IBCS Re-Certification until February 2028!
entry.content:
In an era where AI increasingly generates and surfaces insights, clarity, consistency, and trust in how data is presented becomes more critical. The International Business Communication Standards (IBCS) provides a global framework to ensure business information is visualized in a way that is consistent, comparable, and universally interpretable.

We’re excited to share that SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) in Business Data Cloud (BDC) has been re-certified by IBCS until February 2028. This re-certification reflects SAP's continued commitment to:

  1. Standardized Visual Communication across ALL Analytics Experiences
  2. Reducing Cognitive Load for Consumers
  3. Enabling Faster and Confident Decision

Continuous Improvements toward IBCS Excellence

IBCS re-certification is driven by a series of targeted enhancements that improve how users interpret and interact with data, strengthening consistency, readability, and comparability across analytics experiences. As part of the February 2028 re-certification, SAP Analytics Cloud within Business Data Cloud introduces several key improvements, which we explore in more detail below.


Sort with 100% Stacked Bar Charts

We now support sorting for 100% stacked bar charts, allowing users to order categories based on values or measures. This enhancement improves data analysis by making trends and comparisons easier to interpret.

 


Grid Lines for Improved Readability

We’ve introduced Grid Lines which can be toggled on and off via the Designer in the Charts Show / Hide settings. It can help improve the readability of the Chart and make it easier to interpret data points that have their data labels hidden due to collision. There is an option to update the color of the Grid Lines via the Styling Panel.


+/- Indicators for Table Comparisons

Within Tables, Designers can now configure the +/- indicators for comparison calculations, making variances immediately distinguishable and clearly communicated at a glance.

 

Configurable Separator Line Color and Axis / Separator Line Thickness

Separator line and axis line thickness can now be controlled, and separator line color can be configured. Axis line color remains configurable, providing consistent flexibility in defining visual structure and hierarchy.


Improved Padding for Data Label Backgrounds

Refined data label background padding improves readability and ensures values remain clear and legible, even in compact visualizations.


Enhanced Axis Title Configuration

For Viewers to have better context, Designers can now add Axis Titles which reflects the name of the bound object. It helps in understanding what data is being represented on each axis.

For the Axis Title, we allow the configure of the color and position. For the X-Axis Title we support a left, center, and right alignment while for the Y-Axis we support a centre and top alignment.

 


Adjust Bar Width at Widget Level

You can now adjust the bar width at the widget level, instead of having to use Chart Scaling, giving designers greater control over the appearance of individual bar and column charts.

Setting the widget-level bar width overrides chart scaling bar width and default bar width settings for the chart. All other chart scaling settings are still applied.

 

Expanded Number Format Settings in Themes (Tables & Geo)

Extended number formatting options within themes ensure consistent representation of values across tables and geo visualizations, strengthening enterprise-wide standardization.

Why This Matters AND What Comes Next

IBCS is not just a design guideline, it is a foundation for trusted, scalable analytics. With this re-certification, SAC in BDC continues to:

  1. Deliver Consistent and Interpretable Insights
  2. Enable Consumers to Build Mental Models for Faster Decisions with Confidence 
  3. Provide a Standardized Layer for Analytics in AI-driven and Application-Embedded Scenarios

With this, it is important to:

  1. Revisit YOUR Dashboards and Align them with IBCS Best Practices
  2. Leverage these Enhancements to Standardize how Insights are Communicated Across YOUR Organization

entry.title: Access Issue - SAP HANA Cloud on BTP Trial Account
entry.content:
I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to seek urgent assistance regarding an authorization issue I am experiencing with SAP HANA Cloud Central on my SAP BTP Trial account.

─────────────────────────────
ISSUE SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────
Despite having the SAP HANA Cloud subscription active and the required roles assigned in BTP Cockpit, I receive a "Not Authorized" error when attempting to access SAP HANA Cloud Central.

The error message reads:

"You are not authorized to view this content due to missing roles for the selected identity provider.
Please add the required SAP HANA Cloud role(s) to your user in SAP BTP Cockpit or contact your administrator."

─────────────────────────────
SCREENSHOT 1 – Subscription (Attached)

─────────────────────────────
The following roles have been assigned to my user under the 'hana-cloud' and 'subaccount-admin-api-xsuaa' applications:

hana-cloud application:
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Administrator → SAP HANA Cloud Instance Administrator
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Data_Publisher_Administrator → SAP HANA Cloud Data Publisher Administrator
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Data_Publisher_Viewer → SAP HANA Cloud Data Publisher Viewer
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Security_Administrator → SAP HANA Cloud Instance Security Administrator
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Viewer → SAP HANA Cloud Viewer

subaccount-admin-api-xsuaa application:
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Public_API_Administrator
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Public_API_Data_Publisher_Administrator
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Public_API_Data_Publisher_Viewer
• SAP_HANA_Cloud_Public_API_Viewer

─────────────────────────────
SCREENSHOT 2 – SUBSCRIPTION STATUS (Attached)


─────────────────────────────
The SAP HANA Cloud subscription is active under my subaccount:
• Application: SAP HANA Cloud
• Plan: tools
• Status: Subscribed
• Last Updated: April 7, 2026

─────────────────────────────
SCREENSHOT 3 – ERROR SCREEN (Attached)

 

 

 

 

 

─────────────────────────────
Upon launching SAP HANA Cloud Central, I am presented with a "Not Authorized" screen, referencing missing roles for the selected identity provider — despite the roles listed above being assigned.

Thank you for your time and support. I look forward to your response.

Thanks

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - API based on OAuth
entry.content:
There are lots of relevant help document and comprehensive blog post on this topic. I just try to summerize the process.

1. Create a system user. Tcode SU01

2. Enable OAuth for the OData API

This is for OData v2

tcode: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

 

This is for OData v4

tcode: /IWFND/V4_ADMIN

select the project, then select service line, then click on the oauth button. check the flag in the popup dialog. 

3. Maintain OAuth2 Client ID for the system user

tcode: SOAUTH2

This is a view maintained

Please copy the token endpoint URL generated in the view. 

For the client authentication section, please select SSL Client Certificate as well, since the 3rd party client system might not want to use basic auth to retrieve the token. 

4. Maintain Scope for the Client ID

Still in the same view, go to the section Scope Assignment. You can add the scope ID to the table list. Please be aware that only the APIs whose OAuth has been enabled have the scope ID to be selected here. If you cannot find the scope ID in the search help,  please check the configuration in step 2.

 

5. Test in POSTMAN

This is the step retrieving OAuth2 token

This is the step using the token for consuming the API

Conclusion

SAP API does support OAuth2 Authentication. You should try to avoid using basic auth for exposing these APIs.

entry.title: Attachment and note Support in App Manage Automatic Payments
entry.content:
Enhancing Executive Approval Experience:

Why Attachment and Note Support Matters in Manage Automatic Payments

In today’s ERP cloud landscape, automation alone is not enough. What truly defines enterprise readiness is whether an end-to-end business process works seamlessly across all user roles, including the most senior decision makers.

This is exactly why the new SAP Influence idea
👉 “Attachment and Note Support in Manage Automatic Payments”
(described in https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/370881/?section=sectionDetails)
is so critical.

The Bigger Picture: Automatic Payments Are an End-to-End Process

The Manage Automatic Payments app plays a central role in financial operations. From proposal generation to payment execution, it is designed to support highly automated scenarios.

However, in real customer environments, the process does not end with payment creation.

Automatic payments typically trigger multi-level approval workflows, involving:

  • Finance managers
  • CFOs
  • CEOs
  • In some cases, company owners or board-level executives

These approvals are not formalities. They are compliance-critical, audit-relevant, and decision-heavy steps.

The Gap Today: Missing Context During Approval

For executive approvers, context is everything.

When approving high-value or sensitive payment runs, they often need:

  • Supporting attachments (e.g. contracts, invoices, audit documents)
  • Clear notes or explanations describing:
    • Why the payment is triggered
    • What exceptions exist
    • What risks or urgencies apply

Without attachment and note support directly in Manage Automatic Payments, approvers face real challenges:

  • They must leave the app and search for context elsewhere
  • They rely on offline communication (emails, meetings, screenshots)
  • Decision-making becomes slower, fragmented, and less transparent

This breaks the promise of end-to-end automation and weakens trust in the digital approval process.

Why This Matters Even More for Executive Users

CFOs, CEOs, and business owners are often:

  • Infrequent ERP users
  • Extremely sensitive to usability and clarity
  • Focused on risk, compliance, and accountability

At the same time, they are:

  • Key decision makers for purchasing ERP cloud solutions
  • Strong influencers of customer perception and platform adoption

If their approval experience feels incomplete or inconvenient, it directly impacts:

  • Confidence in SAP’s enterprise capabilities
  • Acceptance of further automation
  • Willingness to expand ERP cloud usage

In other words, their experience matters disproportionally.

The Idea: Attachment and Note Support in Manage Automatic Payments

The proposed idea introduces:

  • ✅ Attachment support to provide formal documentation
  • ✅ Note support to add business context and explanations

This enhancement would:

  • Strengthen auditability and compliance
  • Improve transparency and decision quality
  • Enable smoother, faster executive approvals
  • Preserve the integrity of the end-to-end automatic payment flow

Most importantly, it aligns perfectly with how customers actually operate their financial approval processes.

High Impact, High Visibility

This is not a niche requirement.

It is a cross-cutting improvement that:

  • Touches finance operations
  • Influences approval workflows
  • Impacts C-level experience
  • Shapes customer perception of SAP ERP Cloud

For these reasons, Attachment and Note Support deserves high priority in the evolution of Manage Automatic Payments.

Call to Action

If you believe that:

  • End-to-end processes matter
  • Executive users deserve first-class UX
  • Automatic payments should truly be enterprise-ready

👉 Please Vote to Idea #370881 on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

Together, we can ensure that Manage Automatic Payments delivers not only automation, but also confidence, clarity, and trust—from operational users all the way up to the CXO.

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
@Basis_Sri 

I am afraid its not that simple.

Are you really using the SAP HCM compatibility Pack features? Then you need to migrate to designated alternative functionalities SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Human Capital Management for SAP S/4HANA (SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA). 

Now, if you are not using SAP HCM compatibility pack feature in your business then you could remove the unwatend/old entry in PA0000 table as this could be a false positive (you might have used it previously once or twice, by mistake).

But, if you using this CP feature and simply remove the PA0000 table entry then you would still be violating the rules. Please note that these check conditions are simply to determine the usage relevancy to customers. Nullifying the relevance check would not nullify the feature usage! SAP could take legal action if customers use CP features beyond their use rights expiry.

BR,

Shwethamani M

entry.title: Moving from Hub Gateway to Direct S/4HANA OData V4: A Customized Reusable RAP Processing Framework
entry.content:
In SAP landscapes using a Gateway Hub architecture, OData services are typically exposed through a separate Gateway system, with data processing handled in the backend S/4HANA system via RFC calls. While this architecture has been widely used, it introduces additional system dependencies and layers of communication.

With the adoption of ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) and OData V4, services can now be exposed directly from S/4HANA, allowing developers to simplify the architecture and implement more structured service processing patterns.

In this article, I share a framework concept I have used in my developments to organize how requests to RAP Custom Entities are handled. The approach introduces a reusable processing structure for request handling, data extraction, transformation, and response construction.

This concept is intended to improve maintainability, extensibility, and consistency when building RAP-based services, using existing ABAP objects.

End-to-End Implementation

The framework is implemented as a base processing class that contains the common request handling logic such as entity configuration loading, request context extraction, data transformation, and response construction.

This class is not intended to be used directly. Instead, it is designed to be subclassed for each specific service implementation, where entity-specific logic such as data extraction and transformations can be implemented.

To better understand how the framework works, let's walk through the runtime execution flow when a client calls the OData V4 service.

Custom Entity Implementation

@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Order Details (OData V4)'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

@ObjectModel.query: {
  implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_V4_SALESORDER_QUERY'
}

define root custom entity ZCE_SalesOrderDetails
{
  key SalesOrder        : vbeln;
  key SalesOrderItem    : posnr;
      Material          : matnr;
      Customer          : kunnr;
      OrderQuantity     : abap.quan(13,3);
      OrderUnit         : meins;
      NetPrice          : abap.curr(15,2);
      Currency          : waers;
      CreatedBy         : syuname;
      CreatedOn         : abap.dats;
      DeliveryDate      : abap.dats;

      _ScheduleLines : composition [0..*] of ZCE_SalesOrderSchedule;
}

 

Using the Framework Class as a Superclass

The class ZSV00CL_CE_V2TOV4_CONVERSION acts as a generic processing framework for RAP Custom Entities. It provides reusable logic for handling the complete request lifecycle, including request handling, data retrieval orchestration, payload extraction, entity transformation, and response construction.

To use this framework for a specific business entity, a child class must be created that inherits from the framework class. For example: 

ZCL_V4_SALESORDER_QUERY
 
This class is defined as:
CLASS zcl_v4_salesorder_query DEFINITION
PUBLIC
INHERITING FROM zsv00cl_ce_v2tov4_conversion
FINAL
CREATE PUBLIC.

By inheriting from the framework class, the child class automatically gains access to the entire processing pipeline implemented in the superclass, including the implementation of the RAP interface method:

 
IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER~SELECT

This means the child class does not need to reimplement the standard request processing logic.

RAP Custom Entity Processing Framework – Method Responsibilities

 

MethodLayerWhen It Is CalledPurposeKey Actions

MethodLayerWhen its calledPurposeKey Actions
IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER~SELECTRAP Entry LayerTriggered when OData V4 service is calledEntry point for RAP query processingChecks if data is requested, loads entity configurations, and starts request handling
LOAD_ENTITY_CONFIGURATIONSConfiguration LayerImmediately after SELECTLoads metadata configuration for entitiesDefines entity mapping, source entity set, source/target types, and field mappings
HANDLE_DATA_REQUESTProcessing ControllerAfter configurations are loadedMain orchestrator for request executionCalls context extraction, determines entity type, and triggers fetch + transformation logic
GET_REQUEST_CONTEXTContext LayerEarly in request handlingExtracts request parameters from RAP requestReads filters, paging info, entity name, and key values
IS_ROOT_ENTITYRouting LayerAfter context extractionDetermines whether request is root or child entityControls whether data must be fetched or extracted from existing payload
HANDLE_DATA_FETCHData Retrieval LayerWhen root entity is requestedRetrieves data from backend sourcesBuilds request keys, identifies main source entity set, and triggers backend retrieval (RFC / OData V2 / API)
CALL_RFC_FOR_POST (optional)Integration LayerDuring data fetch if backend logic neededCalls external logic such as RFC or APIUsed for backend system communication
EXTRACT_DATAPayload LayerAfter backend data is retrievedExtracts entity-specific data from payload containerDynamically unwraps source entity data into ABAP structures/tables
HANDLE_ENTITY_CONVERSIONS_GETTransformation ControllerAfter data extractionControls transformation processDetermines if source data is structure or table and calls appropriate conversion method
TRANSFORM_STRUCTURE_DATATransformation LayerWhen source payload is a structureConverts single record structure from V2 → V4Applies field mapping and conversion exits
TRANSFORM_TABLE_DATATransformation LayerWhen source payload is a tableConverts multiple records from V2 → V4Iterates over rows and applies field mapping
CONVERSION_EXITBusiness Logic LayerDuring transformationApplies custom logic for specific fieldsHandles timestamp conversion, code mapping, calculated fields
SET_RESPONSE_DATAResponse LayerFinal step before returning resultSends processed data back to RAP frameworkSets data and total record count in response object

 

High-Level Runtime Flow

 

Detailed Method Execution Sequence

Below is the exact order of method execution when the OData V4 service is called.

Step 1 — RAP Query Trigger

When a consumer calls the Custom Entity OData V4 service, RAP invokes :

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

 Example implementation:

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

  IF io_request->is_data_requested( ).

    load_entity_configurations( ).

    handle_data_request(
      ii_request  = io_request
      ii_response = io_response ).

  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

What happens here?

  1. RAP checks if data is requested.
  2. Entity mappings are loaded.
  3. The main processing method is called.

Step 2 - Loading Configurations

The method initializes the processing setup for the requested entity. It determines the entity being requested and loads the relevant configuration that defines how the framework should retrieve, process, and transform the data.

This step allows the framework to dynamically control entity-specific logic while keeping the main request handling flow generic and reusable.

Method: 

LOAD_ENTITY_CONFIGURATIONS

Example:

gt_entity_config = VALUE #( ( entity_id       = 'oDataV4 Entity 1 '
                              entity_set      = 'oDataV2 Entity Set'
                              src_type        = 'Table Type for oDataV2 Entity 1'
                              trg_type        = 'Table Type for oDataV4 Entity 1'
                              src_type_structure = abap_true
                              src_is_entity_set = abap_true
                              field_map       = VALUE ty_abap_map_tab(  ( src_field = 'oData V2 Field 1'  trg_field = 'oData V4 Field 1' )
                                                                        ( src_field = 'oData V2 Field 2'  trg_field = 'oData V4 Field 2'  ) ) )
                            ( entity_id       = 'oDataV4 Entity 2 '
                              entity_set      = 'oDataV2 Entity Set'
                              src_type        = 'Table Type for oDataV2 Entity 2'
                              trg_type        = 'Table Type for oDataV4 Entity 2'
                              src_type_structure = abap_true
                              src_is_entity_set = abap_true
                              field_map       = VALUE ty_abap_map_tab( ( src_field = 'oData V2 Field 1' trg_field = 'oData V4 Field 1' )
                                                                       ( src_field = 'oData V2 Field 2' trg_field = 'oData V4 Field 2' ) ) ) .

Step 2 — Request Context Extraction

The framework extracts important request parameters.

Method called:

GET_REQUEST_CONTEXT

Example:

DATA(ls_context) = get_request_context( ii_request ).
The context contains:
  • Filters
  • Page size
  • Entity ID
  • Key values
Structure example:
Entity ID
Filter Conditions
Paging Information
Keys

 

Step 3 — Identify Root Entity

The framework checks whether the request is for a root entity or a child entity. You can write your own logic here on how the root can be determined. 

Method: 

IS_ROOT_ENTITY
Why is this needed?

Because root entities trigger the initial data retrieval, while child entities use already available payload data.

 

Step 4 — Fetch Source Data

If the request is for the root entity, the following method is executed:

HANDLE_DATA_FETCH

 Example implementation:

METHOD handle_data_fetch.

  build_keys(
    iv_fieldname = 'FIELD_ID'
    iv_value     = 'FIELD_VALUE' ).

  is_main_source_entity_set( ).

ENDMETHOD.
 Responsibilities of this method:
  • Build request keys
  • Determine the main source entity set
  • Trigger backend data retrieval (custom implementation)

    This method is typically redefined in subclasses.

Step 5 — Convert Entity Data

Next, the framework converts the source payload to the target entity.

Method:

HANDLE_ENTITY_CONVERSIONS_GET

This method performs the main transformation workflow.

Example: 
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_t_output_data> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_t_source_data> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_source_data> TYPE any.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_output_data> TYPE any.
    DATA: lr_data TYPE REF TO data.

    " Extract source data for the  entity
    DATA(lr_child_data) = extract_data( is_context-entity_id ).
    CHECK lr_child_data IS BOUND.

    READ TABLE gt_entity_config INTO DATA(ls_entity_config) WITH KEY entity_id          = is_context-entity_id
                                                                     src_is_entity_set  = mv_main_source_entity_set.
    CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

    IF ls_entity_config-src_type_structure = abap_true.
      ASSIGN lr_child_data->* TO <lfs_source_data>.
    ELSE.
      ASSIGN lr_child_data->* TO <lfs_t_source_data>.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(lr_transformed_data) = COND #( WHEN ls_entity_config-src_type_structure = abap_true AND <lfs_source_data> IS ASSIGNED
                                          THEN transform_structure_data(  iv_entity_id   = is_context-entity_id
                                                                          iv_source_data = <lfs_source_data> )
                                        WHEN <lfs_t_source_data> IS ASSIGNED
                                          THEN transform_table_data(  iv_entity_id   = is_context-entity_id
                                                                      iv_source_data = <lfs_t_source_data> ) ).

    CHECK lr_transformed_data IS BOUND.

    IF ls_entity_config-src_type_structure = abap_true.
      CREATE DATA lr_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF (ls_entity_config-trg_type).

      ASSIGN lr_transformed_data->* TO <lfs_output_data>.
      ASSIGN lr_data->* TO <lfs_t_output_data>.

      CHECK <lfs_output_data> IS ASSIGNED AND <lfs_t_output_data> IS ASSIGNED.

      APPEND <lfs_output_data> TO  <lfs_t_output_data>.

    ELSE.
      ASSIGN lr_transformed_data->* TO <lfs_t_output_data>.
      CHECK <lfs_t_output_data> IS ASSIGNED.
    ENDIF.

    set_response_data( ii_response = ii_response
                       it_data     = <lfs_t_output_data> ).

    IF <lfs_source_data> IS  ASSIGNED.
      UNASSIGN <lfs_source_data>.
    ENDIF.
    IF <lfs_t_output_data> IS ASSIGNED.
      UNASSIGN <lfs_t_output_data>.
    ENDIF.
    IF <lfs_t_output_data> IS ASSIGNED.
      UNASSIGN <lfs_t_output_data>.
    ENDIF.
Execution inside the method:
 

Step 6 — Extract Payload Data

Source data is extracted from the payload container.

Method:

EXTRACT_DATA

Example:

    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_t_reftable> TYPE any.

    DATA(lv_type) = VALUE #( gt_entity_config[ entity_id          = iv_entity_id
                                               src_is_entity_set  = mv_main_source_entity_set ]-src_type OPTIONAL ).

    DATA(lv_entity_set) = VALUE #( gt_entity_config[  entity_id         = iv_entity_id
                                                      src_is_entity_set = mv_main_source_entity_set ]-entity_set OPTIONAL ).

    CREATE DATA rr_data TYPE (lv_type).
    ASSIGN rr_data->* TO <lfs_t_reftable>.

    IF <lfs_t_reftable> IS ASSIGNED.
      unwrap( EXPORTING it_data        = VALUE #( gt_payload[ entity_set_name = lv_entity_set ]-data OPTIONAL )
                        iv_entity_set  = lv_entity_set
              IMPORTING ev_data        = <lfs_t_reftable> ).
      UNASSIGN <lfs_t_reftable>.
    ENDIF.
 This returns a dynamic data reference.

Step 7 — Transform the Data

The framework determines whether the payload is:

  • A structure
  • A table

Structure Conversion

Method:
TRANSFORM_STRUCTURE_DATA
Example:
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_source> TYPE any,
                   <lfs_target> TYPE any.

    DATA: lr_source_ref TYPE REF TO data,
          lr_target_ref TYPE REF TO data.

    READ TABLE gt_entity_config INTO DATA(ls_entity_cfg) WITH KEY entity_id         = iv_entity_id
                                                                  src_is_entity_set = mv_main_source_entity_set.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE DATA lr_source_ref TYPE (ls_entity_cfg-src_type)."Creation of Reference Structure for V2 Structure
    CREATE DATA lr_target_ref TYPE (ls_entity_cfg-trg_type)."Creation of Reference Structure for V4 Structure

    ASSIGN lr_source_ref->* TO <lfs_source>.
    <lfs_source> = CORRESPONDING #( iv_source_data ).

    ASSIGN lr_target_ref->* TO <lfs_target>.

    LOOP AT ls_entity_cfg-field_map ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_field_map>).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT <lfs_field_map>-src_field OF STRUCTURE <lfs_source> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_src_val>).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT <lfs_field_map>-trg_field OF STRUCTURE <lfs_target> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_trg_val>).

      IF <lfs_src_val> IS ASSIGNED AND <lfs_trg_val> IS ASSIGNED.
        DATA(lv_conv_val) = conversion_exit( EXPORTING iv_source     = <lfs_source>
                                                       iv_entity_id  = iv_entity_id
                                                       iv_field_name = <lfs_field_map>-trg_field ).

        <lfs_trg_val> = COND #( WHEN lv_conv_val IS NOT INITIAL THEN lv_conv_val
                               ELSE <lfs_src_val> ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    CREATE DATA rr_data LIKE <lfs_target>.
    rr_data->* = <lfs_target>.
 

Table Conversion

Method: 
TRANSFORM_TABLE_DATA​
Example:
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_t_target_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                   <lfs_source_row>     TYPE any,
                   <lfs_target_row>     TYPE any.

    DATA: lr_source_table_ref TYPE REF TO data,
          lr_target_table_ref TYPE REF TO data.

    READ TABLE gt_entity_config INTO DATA(ls_entity_config) WITH KEY  entity_id         = iv_entity_id
                                                                      src_is_entity_set = mv_main_source_entity_set.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE DATA lr_source_table_ref TYPE (ls_entity_config-src_type)."Creation of Reference Table for V2 Structure
    CREATE DATA lr_target_table_ref TYPE (ls_entity_config-trg_type)."Creation of Reference Table for V4 Structure

    ASSIGN lr_source_table_ref->* TO <lfs_t_target_table>.
    <lfs_t_target_table> = CORRESPONDING #( iv_source_data ).

    ASSIGN lr_target_table_ref->* TO <lfs_t_target_table>.

    LOOP AT lr_source_table_ref->* ASSIGNING <lfs_source_row>.
      CREATE DATA lr_target_table_ref LIKE LINE OF <lfs_t_target_table>.
      ASSIGN lr_target_table_ref->* TO <lfs_target_row>.

      LOOP AT ls_entity_config-field_map ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_field_mapping>). "#EC CI_NESTED.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT <lfs_field_mapping>-src_field OF STRUCTURE <lfs_source_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_source_value>).
        ASSIGN COMPONENT <lfs_field_mapping>-trg_field OF STRUCTURE <lfs_target_row> TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_target_value>).
        DATA(lv_converted_value) = conversion_exit( EXPORTING iv_source     = <lfs_source_row>
                                                              iv_entity_id  = iv_entity_id
                                                              iv_field_name = <lfs_field_mapping>-src_field ).

        <lfs_target_value> = COND #( WHEN lv_converted_value IS NOT INITIAL THEN lv_converted_value
                                    ELSE <lfs_source_value> ).

      ENDLOOP.

      APPEND <lfs_target_row> TO <lfs_t_target_table>.
    ENDLOOP.

    CREATE DATA rr_data LIKE <lfs_t_target_table>.
    rr_data->* = <lfs_t_target_table>.

Step 8 — Apply Custom Field Conversions

Before assigning values, the framework checks for custom conversions.

Method:

CONVERSION_EXIT
Example:
    "Redefine and write your own conversions. Example:If any logic was written in D24 for a field, you can write it here.
    CASE iv_entity_id.
      WHEN 'Entity ID 1'.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT iv_field_name OF STRUCTURE iv_source TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_source_value>).
        IF iv_field_name = 'FIELD NAME'.
          rv_field_value = 'Custom Logic' ##NO_TEXT.
        ENDIF.
      WHEN OTHERS.
    ENDCASE.

This allows transformations such as:

  • Timestamp conversion
  • Code translation
  • Business logic fields

Step 9 — Send Response Back to RAP

Finally, the transformed data is sent to the RAP response object.

Method:

SET_RESPONSE_DATA
 Example:
    ii_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( it_data ) ).
    ii_response->set_data( it_data ).
 RAP then returns the result to the OData V4 service consumer.

 

Key Takeaway

The framework follows a layered execution approach:

 

LayerResponsibility
RAP LayerEntry point for OData V4 request
Context LayerExtract request parameters
Data LayerRetrieve V2 payload
Transformation LayerConvert V2 → V4
Response LayerReturn RAP response
 
This design ensures the framework is:
  • Reusable
  • Extensible
  • Entity independent
  • Compatible with RAP architecture

entry.title: Re: How to delete all private bookmarks in SAC Planning S...
entry.content:
@PM8 

Unfortunately, private bookmarks in SAP Analytics Cloud are designed to be user-specific, so there is no option for administrators to delete them centrally. There is also no setting available to bulk delete private bookmarks for all users.

As a workaround, you can consider the following options:

  1. Request all users to manually delete their private bookmarks (Go to Bookmarks → Manage → Delete)
  2. Create a copy of the story, share the new version with users, and ask them to switch to the updated story.

entry.title: Re: Is This Really How Automatic Valuation Should Work in...
entry.content:
Yes, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, quantitative Master Inspection Characteristics (MICs) with tolerance limits do not automatically valuate at the MIC level, even if all sample results are within tolerance. This is currently intended system behavior and a limitation compared to on‑premise SAP QM, where automatic valuation can be configured more flexible.

If your business requires automatic MIC valuation for quantitative characteristics, this is not yet supported in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The best path is to:

  • Document the manual step in your process design.
  • Raise the requirement through SAP’s Influence Program or roadmap discussions.
  • Consider whether qualitative MICs or simplified inspection plans can reduce manual effort.

entry.title: Joule Setup for SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
This blog will guide you through setting up Joule with SAP Field Service Management. Joule, the Business AI copilot integrated within SAP’s ecosystem, enhances SAP Field Service Management (FSM) by bringing intelligence, automation, and real-time insights to service operations. 

By combining Joule’s Informational and Transactional capabilities with SAP FSM’s operational capabilities, organizations can streamline field service activities from planning and scheduling to execution and feedback with greater accuracy and efficiency. We will be detailing all the scenarios that you can use Joule with FSM.  

Before we get started with the Joule integration with SAP FSM, it’s important to understand the overall Joule integration for your organization. Please look at the blogs below:  

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation: 

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..) 
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...)  
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide 
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach  
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain 
  • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide 
  • Joule Setup for SAP Integrated Product Development 
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide 
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start 
  • Analytical Insights 
  • Analytical Insights in Joule – Setup Guide 
  • Joule Integration with SAP Ariba - Setup Guide 
  • Joule Integration with SAP Concur - Setup Guide 
  • Joule integration with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide 

While we plan your Joule integration, it’s important to know your landscape and how you would like to activate Joule. 

 

(Image 1) 

 

We will explain both the processes, with Section 1 focusing on first-time setup and Section 2 focusing on Joule with an existing setup, to make it easy for our customers. For both these sections, SAP Cloud Identity Service setup is common with your SAP Signavio systems.   

 Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP): 

  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP) 
  • SAP Asset Performance Management 
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management 
  • and more to be added towards General Availability 
  • You are currently here – Step by Step Guide - Joule Setup for SAP Field Service Management 

**************************************************************************** 

It is always recommended to check the SAP Official Help Page for Joule Integration with SAP Field Service Management and check the Joule Capabilities (user interactions with FSM)  

To begin with, let us take a look at the prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP Field Service Management.  

  • SAP Field Service Management system 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform with Joule Entitlements 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is working with your SAP Field Service Management system  
  • How to synchronize users from Cloud Identity Services to SAP Field Service (prerequisite) Management SAP NOTES 
  • How to Log in to SAP Field Service Management with SAP Cloud Identity Services (OpenID Connect) (prerequisite) SAP Notes 

 *************************************************************************************

Integrating Joule  Setup Plan:   

  • Section 1: New Field Service Management setup with no prior Joule setup in your organization.   
  • Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for Field Service Management. 

************************************************************************************* 

Section 1:  Covers 

 (A) Creating a Subaccount & Choosing Data Center 

(B) Enable Cloud Foundry  

(C) Trust configuration between BTP and Cloud Identity Service  

(D)  Adding Entitlements  

(E) Runing the Joule Booster 

(F) Configure trusted domains in identity authentication for the SAP FSM 

(G) Configure a trusted domain in SAP BTP Cockpit for the same SAP FSM 

(H) See Joule in action with FSM 

 In detail explanation below:  

 

(A) Creating a Subaccount & Choosing Datacenter 

Check this blog and follow step No – 5  

(Image 2) 

Check this blog and follow step No – 4  

(Image 3) 

 

(B) Enable Cloud Foundry 

 Once Subaccount Created -> Under Cloud Foundry Environment -> Click on “Enable Cloud Foundry Environment”  -> Select Plan “Standard” -> Click on Create 

 

(Image 4) 

 

 (C) Trust configuration between BTP and Cloud Identity Service 

 

Check this blog and follow step No – 7 

 

(Image 5) 

 

(Image 6) 

 

(Image 7) 

 (D)  Adding Entitlements 

 Check this blog and follow step No – 8   

 

(Image 😎 

 

(E) Runing the Joule Booster for FSM (Field Service Management)  

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 10 explains the process. For simplification, we have the simplified steps and images below.   

 

  • Click on Global Account -> Select Booster -> Search for Joule -> Run the Booster  

 

(Image 9) 

 

The system will validate the entitlements required. Once it’s green, you can click on Next   

Select the SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Next  

 

(Image 10) 

 

Select Integrations, you will be able to see the options for SAP Field Service Management , select it, and click on Next  

 

(Image 11) 

 

(Image 12) 

Once you select the FSM System, you can click on Next, review the selections, and click on Finish. The booster will take 5-9 minutes to activate Joule and set up the integrations with your FSM system.   

(Image 13) 

 

(Image 14) 

 

(Image 15) 

 

Joule will now return the success message after the integrations are completed. You can validate this setup by navigating to Systems and Formation, searching for the Joule formation that will be created for your setup, as shown below.   

 

 (Image 16) 

 

(F) Configure trusted domains in identity authentication for the SAP FSM 

  • For more information, refer to Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication Tenant.  

Open Cloud Identity Services -> click on Applications & Resources -> Tenant Settings-> Customization-> Trusted Domains -> Add 

 

(Image 17) 

 

(G) Configure a trusted domain in SAP BTP Cockpit for the same SAP FSM 

 

  • For more information, refer to Configure Trusted Domain in SAP BTP.  

Open Sub Account -> Security -> Settings -> Trusted Domains -> Add   

 

(Image 18) 

 

(H) See Joule in action with FSM 

 

(Image 19) 

 

************************************************************************************* 

Section 2: Joule Setup with existing Subaccount 

 You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for Field Service Management 

You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for FSM (Field Service Management). In this option, we will show you how to activate FSM in the context of an existing Joule formation. Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes -> click on Formations   

Search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, “I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below”. You can click on Include Systems.    

 

(Image 20) 

 In the new window, filter for SAP Field Service Management and click on Next step.   

 

(Image 21) 

 

Enable Deployment and Joule Icon configuration and click Next 

(Image 22) 

 

Review the final changes 

(Image 23) 

 

Click on Include the system and access the Joule in your FSM system Landscapoe 

(Image 24) 

 

************************************************************************************ 

Thank you for staying connected with our blog and successfully activating Joule for SAP Field Service Management!  

  
We hope you’ve had an exciting experience exploring the power of Joule, your intelligent business companion. From insightful decisions to streamlined processes, we trust you’re already seeing the incredible value Joule brings to your day-to-day work.  

Here’s smarter, faster, and more impactful outcomes powered by Joule! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Certification ideas

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Order Object list change documents log
entry.content:
In SAP Plant Maintenance, the object list in a maintenance order is a tricky area because it can be populated from multiple sources — maintenance item, task list, or even user exits and unfortunately, the system does not always leave a clear change log when objects are derived automatically.

So the suggested approach is

  • Debug the FM IWO_OBJECT_LIST_CREATE during order generation to see the source of the additional equipment.
  • Check IHSG/IHSGU entries for the order to confirm the origin.
  • Review MPOS/MPOB for the maintenance item’s object list definition.
  • Check task list assignment (PLKO/PLPO) to see if multiple technical objects are linked.
  • If unexplained, review active user exits/BAdIs in your system for custom logic adding objects.
  •

entry.title: Joule Setup for SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
This blog will guide you through setting up Joule with SAP Field Service Management. Joule, the Business AI copilot integrated within SAP’s ecosystem, enhances SAP Field Service Management (FSM) by bringing intelligence, automation, and real-time insights to service operations. 

By combining Joule’s Informational and Transactional capabilities with SAP FSM’s operational capabilities, organizations can streamline field service activities from planning and scheduling to execution and feedback with greater accuracy and efficiency. We will be detailing all the scenarios that you can use Joule with FSM.  

Before we get started with the Joule integration with SAP FSM, it’s important to understand the overall Joule integration for your organization. Please look at the blogs below:  

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation: 

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..) 
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...)

While we plan your Joule integration, it’s important to know your landscape and how you would like to activate Joule.

We will explain both the processes, with Section 1 focusing on first-time setup and Section 2 focusing on Joule with an existing setup, to make it easy for our customers. For both these sections, SAP Cloud Identity Service setup is common with your SAP Signavio systems.

To begin with, let us take a look at the prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP Field Service Management.  

  • SAP Field Service Management system 
  • SAP Business Technology Platform with Joule Entitlements 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is working with your SAP Field Service Management system  
  • How to synchronize users from Cloud Identity Services to SAP Field Service (prerequisite) Management SAP NOTES 
  • How to Log in to SAP Field Service Management with SAP Cloud Identity Services (OpenID Connect) (prerequisite) SAP Notes 

 *************************************************************************************

Integrating Joule  Setup Plan:   

  • Section 1: New Field Service Management setup with no prior Joule setup in your organization.   
  • Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for Field Service Management. 

************************************************************************************* 

Section 1:  Covers 

 (A) Creating a Subaccount & Choosing Data Center 

(B) Enable Cloud Foundry  

(C) Trust configuration between BTP and Cloud Identity Service  

(D)  Adding Entitlements  

(E) Runing the Joule Booster 

(F) Configure trusted domains in identity authentication for the SAP FSM 

(G) Configure a trusted domain in SAP BTP Cockpit for the same SAP FSM 

(H) See Joule in action with FSM 

 In detail explanation below:  

 

(A) Creating a Subaccount & Choosing Datacenter 

Check this blog and follow step No – 5  

(Image 2) 

Check this blog and follow step No – 4  

(Image 3) 

 

(B) Enable Cloud Foundry 

 Once Subaccount Created -> Under Cloud Foundry Environment -> Click on “Enable Cloud Foundry Environment”  -> Select Plan “Standard” -> Click on Create 

 

(Image 4) 

 

 (C) Trust configuration between BTP and Cloud Identity Service 

 

Check this blog and follow step No – 7 

 

(Image 5) 

 

(Image 6) 

 

(Image 7) 

 (D)  Adding Entitlements 

 Check this blog and follow step No – 8   

 

(Image 😎 

 

(E) Runing the Joule Booster for FSM (Field Service Management)  

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 10 explains the process. For simplification, we have the simplified steps and images below.   

 

  • Click on Global Account -> Select Booster -> Search for Joule -> Run the Booster  

 

(Image 9) 

 

The system will validate the entitlements required. Once it’s green, you can click on Next   

Select the SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Next  

 

(Image 10) 

 

Select Integrations, you will be able to see the options for SAP Field Service Management , select it, and click on Next  

 

(Image 11) 

 

(Image 12) 

Once you select the FSM System, you can click on Next, review the selections, and click on Finish. The booster will take 5-9 minutes to activate Joule and set up the integrations with your FSM system.   

(Image 13) 

 

(Image 14) 

 

(Image 15) 

 

Joule will now return the success message after the integrations are completed. You can validate this setup by navigating to Systems and Formation, searching for the Joule formation that will be created for your setup, as shown below.   

 

 (Image 16) 

 

(F) Configure trusted domains in identity authentication for the SAP FSM 

  • For more information, refer to Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication Tenant.  

Open Cloud Identity Services -> click on Applications & Resources -> Tenant Settings-> Customization-> Trusted Domains -> Add 

 

(Image 17) 

 

(G) Configure a trusted domain in SAP BTP Cockpit for the same SAP FSM 

 

  • For more information, refer to Configure Trusted Domain in SAP BTP.  

Open Sub Account -> Security -> Settings -> Trusted Domains -> Add   

 

(Image 18) 

 

(H) See Joule in action with FSM 

 

(Image 19) 

 

************************************************************************************* 

Section 2: Joule Setup with existing Subaccount 

 You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for Field Service Management 

You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for FSM (Field Service Management). In this option, we will show you how to activate FSM in the context of an existing Joule formation. Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes -> click on Formations   

Search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, “I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below”. You can click on Include Systems.    

 

(Image 20) 

 In the new window, filter for SAP Field Service Management and click on Next step.   

 

(Image 21) 

 

Enable Deployment and Joule Icon configuration and click Next 

(Image 22) 

 

Review the final changes 

(Image 23) 

 

Click on Include the system and access the Joule in your FSM system Landscapoe 

(Image 24) 

 

************************************************************************************ 

Thank you for staying connected with our blog and successfully activating Joule for SAP Field Service Management!  

  
We hope you’ve had an exciting experience exploring the power of Joule, your intelligent business companion. From insightful decisions to streamlined processes, we trust you’re already seeing the incredible value Joule brings to your day-to-day work.  

Here’s smarter, faster, and more impactful outcomes powered by Joule! 🚀

entry.title: Re: POD confirmation needed after billing due to Carta Po...
entry.content:
This is a very relevant challenge in Mexico because the Carta Porte CFDI process legally requires billing before goods can leave the warehouse, which flips the usual SAP Proof of Delivery (POD) sequence where, this POD will happen before billing.

In standard SAP copy control (Delivery → Billing), requirement routine 003 (I believe) is the one that validates the POD status before allowing billing. That is why, in your current setup, billing is blocked until POD is confirmed. For your Mexican scenario, where billing must happen first to generate the CFDI/Carta Porte, you can instead assign standard routine 004 in copy control. Routine 004 follows the normal delivery‑related billing process and does not enforce POD validation. This way, billing can be created immediately after delivery creation, while POD (VLPOD) can still be used later purely as a delivery confirmation step.

entry.title: Re: Transparent huge pages are not enabled in HANA
entry.content:
its all in the sap note described

Edit /etc/default/grub

search for the line starting with "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT" and append to this line

    transparent_hugepage=madvise

Save your changes and run

    # grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

After a reboot, the configuration will become active.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Stop notifications sent to S-users at your company ar...
entry.content:
Hi customer,

Please kindly know such kind of notification is S-user management notification, and it's subscribed and managed in the SAP for Me Notification Center. 

If user doesn't want to get such notifications in the future, pls user kindly access https://me.sap.com/home > User profile >  My Settings > tab Support-Related Notifications (direct link: https://me.sap.com/preferences/oslpSubscriptions), then pls switch off the notification type 'User Management'.

Refer to KBA 2530034 - How to manage e-mail, SMS, and/or SAP for Me notifications - SAP for Me.

User may also review the other types of notifications and adjust the subscription. Switch off those unnecessary notifications on the same page. 

Hope this works.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Mass Option to delete "In Preparation" Output Items i...
entry.content:
Good day @sans16 

Thank you for your question.

If the email recipient rule is missing or too specific, the EMAIL output can remain get stuck in the “In Preparation” status and therefore not be sent successfully. This reflects the standard Purchase Order output mechanism which KBA 3325734 explains and provides the related OPD steps you can follow to correct it. So, in your case, the main issue is not EDI trigger itself, but the earlier EMAIL output item that remains in the status “In Preparation” because of the receiver determination is not resolved correctly in Output Parameter Determination.

Regarding your question “Is there any mass option to delete the "In Preparation" output line so i can trigger new Output Lines?”, no, there is no standard mass option to delete Purchase Order output items across many Pos. What the framework gives you is for you to centrally monitor and process through Manage Output Items, but not a bulk delete utility for Purchase Order Outputs that are stuck. So, that means Manage Output Items serves a central overview to view and process multiple Outputs, while Purchase Order deletion is governed by the status of the item. See KBA 3555483 regarding output items with the status “In Preparation” or To Be Output and the possibility for you to delete them, while items in Completed or Error aren’t.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/1c9d0d58fb999d44e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

 

So, in your scenario, the approach I would recommend is:

1. Correct the OPD setup first: Open the Output Parameter Determination, select Purchase order, check both Output Type

and Email Recipient.

Ensure the impacted purchasing organisation, company code, and purchasing group combinations are covered, or maintain a fallback rule if you deem it appropriate.

 

2. In Manage Purchase Orders, handle one affected PO as a validation test, go to the output area, if the EMAIL output item is still in “In Preparation”, delete that item if the delete action is available for the status. 

If you consider deletion not practical in the affected document state, maintain the correct receiver so the output can complete and save the PO. Make an output relevant change and save again to trigger new output determination. See KBA 3555483 for more details on the deletion rule.

 

3. You can use Manage Output Items for monitoring by filtering Purchase Orders output with channel EMAIL and status In Preparation. Use this if you need central visibility and follow up after the OPD correction.

If you need to clear 2500+ existing documents, the key point is that SAP standard does not provide a mass delete function for this specific PO output cleanup. For that reason, after correction OPD, I would recommend you test a small batch first and then if needed open an SAP incident if you require a large scale correction.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris
